title: Update README usage and MCP docs
summary: The README now uses the eve project name, gives concise local run and verification instructions, explains the runtime architecture and Git relationship with Mermaid diagrams, and documents MCP setup for Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and other agents.
userVisibleChange: Developers can understand what eve stores, run it locally, and connect it to common MCP-capable coding agents from one short README.

timeline.title: Rewrote repository README
timeline.summary: Replaced the older README with a shorter guide covering storage, local commands, API, MCP tools, and library APIs.

timeline.title: Added architecture diagrams
timeline.summary: Added Mermaid diagrams for the runtime architecture and how eve complements Git history.

timeline.title: Documented MCP client setup
timeline.summary: Added stdio and HTTP configuration examples for Codex, Claude Code, opencode, and generic MCP clients after checking current docs.

timeline.title: Ran repository verification
timeline.summary: Go tests, UI tests, and the UI production build completed successfully.

decisions.title: Prefer stdio for agent-launched MCP
decisions.rationale: Stdio lets each agent start a local eve process for the target repository without requiring a long-running runtime.

decisions.title: Keep HTTP as the running-runtime option
decisions.rationale: The local runtime already exposes /mcp, so HTTP is useful when eve dev is already serving the UI and API.

decisions.title: Use installed eve command in client snippets
decisions.rationale: Agent configs are usually used from other repositories, so the snippets assume go install ./cmd/eve has put eve on PATH.

validation.output: Vitest ran src/format.test.ts with 3 passing tests.
tsc --noEmit, vite build, and copy-dist completed successfully; Vite reported the existing chunk-size warning.
